reflection.quote: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
reflection.reading:
Thursdays used to be the worst. Not bad enough to be a crisis, not good enough to feel like anything. Just a long stretch of hours to get through until I could justify the first drink. The whole week was scaffolding for the weekend, and the weekend was scaffolding for forgetting the week.

What sobriety gave me, more than anything dramatic, is Thursday. A morning where I actually remember the conversation I had last night. Coffee that tastes like coffee instead of medicine for a hangover. The slow accumulation of doing what I said I'd do — showing up to work, calling my mom back, making the bed — until those small acts stopped feeling like chores and started feeling like the texture of a life. Durant's line about habit isn't really about excellence. It's about identity. You become the thing you keep doing. If you keep drinking, you become someone who drinks. If you keep showing up, you become someone who shows up.

The trick is that Thursday is where it actually happens. Not in the big moments, not in the anniversaries or the breakthroughs. In the unremarkable middle of an unremarkable week, when nobody is watching and there's no reward except the quiet fact that you're still here, still building, still becoming someone you can live with.
reflection.meditation: What small, ordinary thing can I show up for today — not because it's impressive, but because doing it is who I'm becoming?

affirmations: I am building a life one ordinary day at a time, and today counts.
Today I get to be present for whatever happens — the dull parts included.
I am becoming someone I can trust to keep his word, starting with the word I gave myself.

gratitude.prayer: Grateful for a Thursday I can remember. Grateful for the people who stayed while I figured this out, and the ones I've met since who only know me sober. Grateful that the small things — coffee, a clear head, a quiet morning — are no longer things I have to earn back from a hangover. They're just here, waiting, every day I choose to show up.
gratitude.closing: One more day. That's the whole deal.
